sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Standard Liège have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins and 2 losses, including a heavy 4-0 defeat to KAA Gent. SV Zulte Waregem, on the other hand, have also had an inconsistent run with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, though they managed a strong 4-1 victory over KAA Gent recently. Both teams struggle defensively, with neither keeping a clean sheet in their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Standard Liège and SV Zulte Waregem have split the results, with a 2-2 draw in March 2023 and a 3-0 win for Standard Liège in October 2022. Matches between these sides often produce goals, with 9 of the last 10 head-to-head games seeing over 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Standard Liège (10th, 17 points) struggle offensively, ranking 13th in goals scored (13), and have low ball possession (43.2%, 15th). SV Zulte Waregem (8th, 19 points) are more clinical with 21 goals scored, often finding the net late in games (28.6% of goals in the 76-90 minute range). Both teams concede frequently, suggesting a potentially open game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Standard Liège, Rafiki Saïd (3 goals, 2 assists) and Thomas Henry (3 goals, 2 assists) are key threats upfront. SV Zulte Waregem rely heavily on Jeppe Erenbjerg, who leads the league with 7 goals and boasts a high xG overperformance (+2.23).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (40% home win, 25% draw, 35% away win) and recent form, this match appears finely balanced. Standard Liège may have a slight edge at home, but Zulte Waregem’s attacking prowess and late-game threat could exploit defensive frailties. Expect a competitive encounter with goals likely on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Standard Liège and SV Zulte Waregem at Maurice Dufrasne Stadium ended in a goalless draw. Standard Liège dominated possession and created numerous opportunities, showing a clear intent to control the game. On the other hand, SV Zulte Waregem struggled to make an impact in the attacking third, compounded by a significant setback with a player being sent off. This numerical disadvantage has put them on the back foot, making it difficult to mount a consistent challenge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège showcased their superiority with a high percentage of ball possession and frequent entries into the final third. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, allowed them to maintain pressure on the opposition. Despite missing a big chance, they created several promising moments in the penalty area. Players like Mo El Hankouri and Thomas Henry have been central to their attacking efforts, with notable contributions in creating chances and taking shots on target. However, their finishing needs to improve to convert dominance into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team struggles
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem faced an uphill battle in the first half, with minimal time on the ball and very few entries into dangerous areas. Their defensive structure held firm initially, but the red card to a key player severely hampered their ability to compete. Without a single shot on target or a big chance created, their attacking threat was virtually nonexistent. Defensively, they’ve had to dig deep, with players like Laurent Lemoine and Anton Tanghe working hard to clear danger, but sustaining this effort will be challenging with a man down.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half started with SV Zulte Waregem showing some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, this quickly shifted as Standard Liège took control, launching consistent attacking spells from around the midway point of the half. Their persistent pressure in the latter stages of the half saw them dominate proceedings, with several waves of attack that pinned the away side back. In contrast, Zulte Waregem’s attacking momentum faded significantly after the initial minutes, with only sporadic attempts to push forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège’s game plan revolved around maintaining possession and exploiting the wings to create opportunities, which they executed well. Their high pressing and ability to win the ball in advanced areas kept Zulte Waregem under constant pressure. For the away side, the red card forced them into a defensive shell, focusing on compactness to avoid conceding. This numerical disadvantage likely means they’ll continue to prioritize defense in the second half, potentially looking for rare counter-attacking opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the dynamics of the first half, Standard Liège are in a strong position to capitalize on their numerical advantage. They are expected to continue their high-pressing game and focus on breaking down Zulte Waregem’s defense with more clinical finishing. Zulte Waregem, on the other hand, will likely struggle to create meaningful chances with a man down, focusing instead on damage limitation. The home side’s persistence should eventually pay off, and they are favored to find the back of the net in the second half.
